messages.summary: URGENT FIX: The "Gateway Offline" notification appears on EVERY page even when gateway is running. F
messages.originalContent:
URGENT FIX: The "Gateway Offline" notification appears on EVERY page even when gateway is running. Fix it completely.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.

DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, or globals.css.

Task 1: Open src/components/notifications/notification-system.tsx (or wherever the Gateway Offline banner is). Find where it checks gateway status. It currently checks incorrectly. Fix the logic:
- Fetch /api/bridge/status
- If response has gateway.status === 'running' OR services array has a running gateway → gateway IS online → do NOT show warning
- If fetch fails OR gateway not running → show warning
- Add try/catch so if Bridge API is down, show "Connecting..." not "Gateway Offline"

Task 2: The notification "CPU usage has been above 85%" is fake/hardcoded. Remove it. Only show REAL notifications:
- Gateway status (from /api/bridge/status)
- Real CPU alert only if /api/bridge/metrics returns cpu > 85
- Build/deploy status

Task 3: The notification bell in the top bar shows a red badge with count. Make sure the count matches REAL unread notifications only. If no real alerts, badge should be hidden.

Task 4: Also check if there is a NotificationBell.tsx component that duplicates this logic. If yes, consolidate into one system.

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Fix Gateway Offline notification + remove fake alerts" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard
Do all 4 tasks NOW.

tasks.originalDescription: Open src/components/notifications/notification-system.tsx (or wherever the Gateway Offline banner is). Find where it checks gateway status. It currently checks incorrectly. Fix the logic:

microTasks.change: Add try-catch wrapper around gateway status fetch
microTasks.description: Locate the useEffect hook or function that checks gateway status. Find the current fetch call or status check logic. Replace the existing broken condition with a proper async function called checkGatewayStatus(). Inside this function, use try-catch to wrap the fetch('/api/bridge/status') call. Parse the JSON response and store it in a variable called statusData. This is the foundation for proper gateway detection.

microTasks.change: Fix gateway online detection with dual-format check
microTasks.description: Inside the checkGatewayStatus function, after parsing statusData, implement the correct online detection logic. Create a boolean variable isGatewayOnline that checks: (statusData.gateway?.status === 'running') OR (statusData.services?.some(s => s.name === 'gateway' && s.status === 'running')). This handles both possible API response formats. If isGatewayOnline is true, set the gatewayOffline state to false and DO NOT add any gateway warning notification.

microTasks.change: Handle API unreachable with 'Connecting...' state and retries
microTasks.description: In the catch block of checkGatewayStatus, handle the case where the Bridge API itself is unreachable. Instead of immediately showing 'Gateway Offline', set a new state variable connectionStatus to 'connecting'. Add a state: const [connectionStatus, setConnectionStatus] = useState<'connected'|'connecting'|'offline'>('connecting'). In the catch block call setConnectionStatus('connecting') and schedule a retry with setTimeout(checkGatewayStatus, 5000). Only after 3 failed retries should you set connectionStatus to 'offline'.

microTasks.change: Implement retry counter logic for connection failures
microTasks.description: Add a retry counter state: const [retryCount, setRetryCount] = useState(0). In the catch block, increment retryCount. If retryCount >= 3, then set connectionStatus to 'offline' and add the gateway offline notification. If retryCount < 3, keep connectionStatus as 'connecting' and schedule retry. In the success path of the try block, reset retryCount to 0 and set connectionStatus to 'connected'.

microTasks.change: Render different banners based on connectionStatus state
microTasks.description: Update the JSX render section to conditionally show different messages based on connectionStatus state. If connectionStatus === 'connecting', show a subtle blue banner with text 'Connecting to Gateway...' and a spinning loader icon. If connectionStatus === 'offline', show the red warning banner 'Gateway Offline'. If connectionStatus === 'connected', show nothing (no banner). This replaces any hardcoded offline banner that was showing incorrectly.

tasks.originalDescription: The notification "CPU usage has been above 85%" is fake/hardcoded. Remove it. Only show REAL notifications:

microTasks.change: Remove hardcoded CPU usage notification from initial state
microTasks.description: Search for any hardcoded notification array or initial state that contains the text 'CPU usage' or '85%'. This might be in a useState initial value like useState([{type: 'warning', message: 'CPU usage has been above 85%'...}]) or in a const MOCK_NOTIFICATIONS array. Delete this entire hardcoded entry. The initial notifications state should be an empty array: useState<Notification[]>([]).

microTasks.change: Add real CPU metrics check that only alerts when cpu > 85
microTasks.description: Create a new async function called checkRealMetrics() that fetches from '/api/bridge/metrics'. Parse the response JSON. Check if response.cpu exists and is a number greater than 85. Only if cpu > 85, create a real notification object: {id: 'cpu-alert-' + Date.now(), type: 'warning', message: `CPU usage is at ${response.cpu}%`, timestamp: new Date(), read: false}. Add this to notifications state using setNotifications(prev => [...prev.filter(n => !n.id.startsWith('cpu-alert')), newNotification]).

microTasks.change: Add metrics checking to useEffect with 30-second interval
microTasks.description: In the useEffect hook that runs on component mount, call checkRealMetrics() alongside checkGatewayStatus(). Also set up an interval: const metricsInterval = setInterval(checkRealMetrics, 30000) to check metrics every 30 seconds. Add this interval to the cleanup return function: return () => { clearInterval(metricsInterval); clearInterval(statusInterval); }. This ensures real-time CPU monitoring without fake data.

microTasks.change: Audit and remove all mock/fake notification sources
microTasks.description: Remove any other fake/mock notifications in the file. Search for strings like 'demo', 'test', 'mock', 'sample', 'example' in notification messages. Delete any useEffect or function that adds notifications without fetching from a real API endpoint. The only sources of notifications should be: /api/bridge/status (gateway), /api/bridge/metrics (CPU), and /api/deployments or /api/builds (build/deploy status).

tasks.originalDescription: The notification bell in the top bar shows a red badge with count. Make sure the count matches REAL unread notifications only. If no real alerts, badge should be hidden.

microTasks.change: Fix unread count to use dynamic filter on real notifications
microTasks.description: Find the unread count calculation. It should be: const unreadCount = notifications.filter(n => !n.read).length. Make sure this filters the actual notifications state array that only contains real notifications (gateway status, real CPU alerts, real build status). Do NOT use any hardcoded number. If there's a line like const unreadCount = 3 or badge={3}, replace it with the dynamic filter calculation.

microTasks.change: Conditionally render badge only when unreadCount > 0
microTasks.description: Find the Badge component or red dot indicator in the JSX. It likely wraps the Bell icon. Add a conditional render: only show the badge when unreadCount > 0. The code should be: {unreadCount > 0 && <Badge className='absolute -top-1 -right-1 h-5 w-5 rounded-full bg-red-500 text-white text-xs flex items-center justify-center'>{unreadCount}</Badge>}. If unreadCount is 0, the badge element should not render at all (not just be hidden).

microTasks.change: Add markAsRead and markAllAsRead functions
microTasks.description: Add a markAsRead function that updates a notification's read status: const markAsRead = (notificationId: string) => { setNotifications(prev => prev.map(n => n.id === notificationId ? {...n, read: true} : n)); }. Also add markAllAsRead: const markAllAsRead = () => { setNotifications(prev => prev.map(n => ({...n, read: true}))); }. Wire markAllAsRead to a 'Mark all read' button in the dropdown if it exists.

microTasks.change: Render notification list from state only, show empty message if none
microTasks.description:
Ensure the notification dropdown list only displays notifications from the state array. Map through notifications: {notifications.map(notification => ( markAsRead(notification.id)} />))}. If notifications.length === 0, show a message:

No notifications

. Remove any hardcoded list items.

tasks.originalDescription: Also check if there is a NotificationBell.tsx component that duplicates this logic. If yes, consolidate into one system.

microTasks.change: Identify if NotificationBell.tsx exists and has duplicate logic
microTasks.description: Check if this file exists. If it does, examine its contents. It likely has duplicate logic for showing notification count and dropdown. If the file exists and has its own state management for notifications, this creates a conflict with notification-system.tsx. The goal is to have ONE source of truth for notifications. If this file exists, we need to either delete it or convert it to a simple wrapper that uses the main notification system.

microTasks.change: Convert NotificationBell to presentational component with props
microTasks.description: If NotificationBell.tsx exists with duplicate logic, refactor it to be a presentational component only. Remove any useState for notifications, remove any useEffect that fetches status/metrics. Instead, accept props: interface NotificationBellProps { notifications: Notification[]; unreadCount: number; onMarkRead: (id: string) => void; onMarkAllRead: () => void; }. The component should only render UI, not manage state.

microTasks.change: Integrate NotificationBell as child component with props
microTasks.description: If NotificationBell.tsx was converted to presentational, import it into notification-system.tsx and render it with the required props: <NotificationBell notifications={notifications} unreadCount={unreadCount} onMarkRead={markAsRead} onMarkAllRead={markAllAsRead} />. If NotificationBell.tsx doesn't exist or was deleted, keep the bell icon rendering directly in notification-system.tsx as is.

microTasks.change: Ensure navbar uses single notification component import
microTasks.description: Check if navbar.tsx imports NotificationBell separately or has its own notification logic. If it imports both NotificationBell and notification-system separately, remove the NotificationBell import. The navbar should only import and use one notification component: NotificationSystem. Find the JSX where notifications are rendered and ensure only <NotificationSystem /> is used, not both components side by side.

microTasks.change: Create NotificationContext for centralized state management
microTasks.description: Consider creating a NotificationContext if multiple components need notification data. Create the file with: const NotificationContext = createContext<NotificationContextType | null>(null). Export a NotificationProvider that wraps children and manages all notification state (notifications array, checkGatewayStatus, checkRealMetrics, markAsRead). Export a useNotifications hook that returns the context. This centralizes all notification logic and prevents duplicate fetching.

Fix the Dashboard page to be professional and useful.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.
DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, globals.css.

Task 1: Live Task Monitor widget at the top. Currently shows "Sprint 0D" and old data. Fix:
- Fetch from /api/bridge/live-status for current task info
- Show: Current phase name, current task name, progress bar
- Make it clickable → navigates to /dashboard/tasks
- If no active task: show "All tasks complete — waiting for next assignment"

Task 2: Stat Cards row (All Projects, Tasks, Team, Automations, Reports, Security). Fix each:

- Tasks: fetch /api/tasks → show real done/total + completion rate

- "View Projects" → navigate to /projects
- "View Reports" → navigate to /dashboard/reports
- "Generate Report" → navigate to /dashboard/reports with generate dialog
- "Manage Team" → navigate to /dashboard/users

Task 5: Activity Stream. Show real recent events:
- Fetch last 5 git commits from /api/bridge/logs or hardcode last known commits

Task 6: Workflows section. Either show real workflows from /api/workflows or hide the section if empty. "No workflows running" is fine but "Start Workflow" button should navigate to /automations.

Fix the top navigation bar to show correct user info.

tasks.originalDescription: Sign Out button must work:

microTasks.change: Add imports for useRouter and cookie handling
microTasks.description: At the top of the file, add the necessary imports for handling sign out functionality. Add: import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation' if not already imported. Also add: import Cookies from 'js-cookie' (install js-cookie package if needed). Alternatively, you can use document.cookie directly. Also ensure 'use client' directive is at the top of the file since we're using client-side navigation and cookie manipulation.

microTasks.change: Create handleSignOut function to clear cookies and redirect
microTasks.description: Inside the Navbar component function, create a handleSignOut async function. This function should: 1) Delete the auth-token cookie using Cookies.remove('auth-token') or document.cookie = 'auth-token=; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 UTC; path=/;' to clear it. 2) Also clear any other auth-related cookies or localStorage items like localStorage.removeItem('user'). 3) Use the router instance: const router = useRouter() at component level, then call router.push('/login') to redirect. Add try-catch for error handling.

microTasks.change: Attach handleSignOut function to Sign out button onClick
microTasks.description: Find the 'Sign out' menu item in the dropdown menu. It's likely a DropdownMenuItem, button, or div with onClick handler. Attach the handleSignOut function to this element's onClick event: onClick={handleSignOut}. If it's currently a Link component, change it to a button element since sign out is an action not navigation. Example: <DropdownMenuItem onClick={handleSignOut}><LogOut className='mr-2 h-4 w-4' />Sign out</DropdownMenuItem>. Ensure the button has cursor-pointer styling.

microTasks.change: Create/update middleware to protect /dashboard routes
microTasks.description: Open or create the middleware.ts file in the src directory. This middleware should check for the auth-token cookie on protected routes. Import NextResponse from 'next/server' and NextRequest type. Create the middleware function that: 1) Gets the auth-token from request.cookies.get('auth-token'). 2) If the pathname starts with '/dashboard' and no auth-token exists, return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', request.url)). 3) Export const config = { matcher: ['/dashboard/:path*'] } to only run on dashboard routes.

microTasks.change: Create /api/auth/logout endpoint for server-side cookie clearing
microTasks.description: Create an API route for logout as a backup server-side logout option. Create the file with: import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'. Export async function POST() that creates a response with NextResponse.json({ success: true }), then uses response.cookies.set('auth-token', '', { expires: new Date(0), path: '/' }) to clear the cookie server-side. Return the response. This provides a reliable way to clear httpOnly cookies if they're set that way.

messages.originalContent:
Fix the Command Center page to be useful and connected to real actions.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.
DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, globals.css.

Task 1: Status cards at top. Fix each:
- System Health: fetch /api/bridge/status. If gateway running → "Healthy" (green). If not → "Warning" (amber)
- Resource Usage: fetch /api/bridge/metrics. Show real CPU%, Memory%, Disk%
- Active Users: show "1" (Med, OWNER). Remove "12" and "8 active tasks" fake data
- Services Status: fetch /api/bridge/status. List real services: OpenClaw Gateway (running/stopped), Bridge API (running), Next.js (running)

Task 2: Quick Actions — make ALL buttons execute real commands:
- "Restart Gateway": POST /api/bridge/gateway → show success/error toast
- "System Health Check": GET /api/bridge/metrics → show results in a modal
- "View Logs": navigate to /dashboard/monitoring (Logs tab)
- "Clear System Cache": show "Cache cleared" toast (can be cosmetic for now)
- "Backup Memory Files": POST /api/bridge/memory with action=backup → show toast
- "Update All Skills": show "Skills are up to date" toast
- "Optimize Database": show "Optimization complete" toast
- "Export System Logs": download /api/bridge/logs as JSON file
- "Network Connectivity Test": fetch /api/bridge/health → show latency result
- "Disk Space Cleanup": show "Cleanup complete" toast
- "Reset Configuration": show confirmation dialog → "Are you sure?" → do nothing on confirm (safety)

Task 3: Remove the "Terminal" and "Services" tabs if they don't have real content. Keep only "Quick Actions" tab.

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Command Center: real status, working actions" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard/command-center
Do all 3 tasks NOW.

messages.summary: The Memory page at /dashboard/memory is broken. Fix it completely.
messages.originalContent:
The Memory page at /dashboard/memory is broken. Fix it completely.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.
DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, globals.css.

Task 1: The page should load the file tree from Bridge API. On mount:
- Fetch GET /api/bridge/memory
- Response has: tree (array of files/folders), stats (totalFiles, totalFolders, totalSize)
- Display tree on the LEFT side as a file browser (folders expandable, files clickable)
- Display stats in the top cards: Total Files, Projects count, Areas count, Resources count

Task 2: When clicking a file in the tree:
- Fetch GET /api/bridge/memory?path=[filePath]
- Response has: content (string), name, path, size
- Display content in the RIGHT panel
- If file ends with .md: render as formatted text (preserve line breaks, headers)
- If other format: show as monospace plain text
- Show file name and path above the content

Task 3: Add EDIT functionality:
- "Edit" button appears when viewing a file
- Click → content becomes a textarea (editable)
- "Save" button → PUT /api/bridge/memory with body {path: filePath, content: newContent}
- Show success toast on save
- "Cancel" button → reverts to view mode without saving

Task 4: Add NEW FILE button in the header:
- Click → modal with two fields: "File path" (e.g. notes/meeting.md) and "Content" (textarea)
- Submit → PUT /api/bridge/memory with {path, content}
- On success → refresh tree and select the new file
- Validate: path cannot be empty, cannot contain ".."

Task 5: Stats cards at top should show:
- Total Files: real count from API
- Projects: count files in projects/ folder
- Areas: count files in areas/ folder  
- Resources: count files in resources/ folder
- Last Updated: most recent file modification date

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Memory page: file browser, edit, create, real data" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard/memory
Do all 5 tasks NOW.

messages.originalContent: # الرسالة 7: إصلاح Skills Library

Improve the Skills Library page.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.
DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, globals.css.

Task 1: Skills are loading (27-35 skills from Bridge API). Good. But improve the cards:
- Each skill card should show: name, status (active badge), category badge, description (first 100 chars)
- Add which DEPARTMENT uses this skill. Map skills to departments:
  * developer, senior-dev, coding-agent → 💻 Frontend/Backend Dev
  * deep-research-pro, exa-search, tavily-search → 🔬 Research
  * security-scanner, security-audit-toolkit → 🛡️ Security
  * critical-code-reviewer → 🔍 QA
  * auto-continue, task-planner → 📋 Planning
  * self-improving-agent → 📈 Self-Improvement
- Show department icon badge on each card

Task 2: Click a skill card → open a detail modal:
- Fetch skill details: GET /api/bridge/skills?name=[skillName]
- Show: full name, description, version, category, department, last used date
- If the skill has a SKILL.md content, show it in a scrollable area
- Close button to dismiss

Task 3: "Add Skill" button in header should show a message: "Skills are managed through OpenClaw. Use the Command Center to update skills." with a link to /dashboard/command-center.

Task 4: Category filter should work with real data:
- "all" shows everything
- "development" filters to developer, coding-agent, etc.
- "research" filters to search and research skills
- "security" filters to security skills
- "productivity" filters to auto-continue, task-planner, etc.

Task 5: Stats cards at top:
- Total Skills: real count
- Development: count of dev skills
- Research: count of research skills
- Security: count of security skills
- Productivity: count of productivity skills

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Skills: department mapping, detail modal, working filters" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard/skills
Do all 5 tasks NOW.

messages.summary: Improve Cron Jobs page with smart job creation.
messages.originalContent:
Improve Cron Jobs page with smart job creation.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.
DESIGN PROTECTION: Do NOT modify sidebar.tsx, navbar.tsx, layout.tsx, globals.css.

Task 1: Stats cards fix:
- Total Jobs: show real count from /api/bridge/cron (should be 3-4)
- Running: count of jobs currently executing
- Failed Today: count of failed jobs today
- Next Job: show time until next execution in human readable format ("in 2h 15m")

Task 2: Job list is working. Improve each job card:
- Show: name, human-readable schedule, last run time, next run time, status badge
- The "Run" toggle should work: POST to /api/bridge/cron/[id]/toggle (create if needed)
- The "..." menu should have: Edit, Delete, Run Now options

Task 3: "New Job" button — make it smart:
- Click → modal with:
  * "What do you want to schedule?" (textarea — natural language)
  * Examples shown below: "Send me a daily progress report at 8 AM", "Check system health every hour", "Backup memory files every Sunday"
- On submit: POST /api/cron/create-smart
- Create this API route that:
  * Takes the natural language description
  * Sends to OpenClaw via Telegram: "Create a cron job: [description]. Respond with: name, cron expression, command."
  * Returns the parsed result to the frontend
  * Shows preview: "Name: daily-report | Schedule: 0 8 * * * (Daily at 8:00 AM) | Command: ..."
  * "Confirm" button → creates the job via openclaw cron create

Task 4: Each job should show the cron expression in human readable form:
- "0 7 * * *" → "Daily at 7:00 AM"
- "0 21 * * *" → "Daily at 9:00 PM"
- "0 10 * * 0" → "Weekly on Sunday at 10:00 AM"
- "*/5 * * * *" → "Every 5 minutes"

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Cron: smart job creation, working toggles, human schedules" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard/cron

tasks.originalDescription: Stats cards fix:

microTasks.change: Make Total Jobs card show real count from API
microTasks.description: In the CronPage component, locate the stats cards section (likely a grid of Card components showing Total Jobs, Running, Failed Today, Next Job). Find the 'Total Jobs' Card component and replace any hardcoded value with dynamic data. Add a useEffect hook that fetches from '/api/bridge/cron', stores the response in state like 'const [jobs, setJobs] = useState([])', then use jobs.length as the value prop for the Total Jobs card. The fetch should be: const res = await fetch('/api/bridge/cron'); const data = await res.json(); setJobs(data.jobs || data || []);

microTasks.change: Make Running card show count of currently executing jobs
microTasks.description: In the same CronPage component, find the 'Running' stats Card. Calculate the running count from the jobs state by filtering: const runningCount = jobs.filter(job => job.status === 'running' || job.isRunning === true).length. Update the Running Card's value prop to display {runningCount}. Make sure this recalculates when jobs state updates by keeping the calculation in the render or using useMemo for optimization.

microTasks.change: Make Failed Today card show count of jobs that failed today
microTasks.description: In CronPage, find the 'Failed Today' stats Card. Calculate failed jobs today by filtering the jobs array: const today = new Date().toDateString(); const failedToday = jobs.filter(job => job.status === 'failed' && job.lastRun && new Date(job.lastRun).toDateString() === today).length. Update the Failed Today Card's value prop to show {failedToday}. Consider also checking job.lastStatus or job.lastResult for failure indicators depending on the API response structure.

microTasks.change: Make Next Job card show human-readable time until next execution

Parse the cron expression by splitting on spaces: const parts = cronExpression.split(' '); const [minute, hour, dayOfMonth, month, dayOfWeek] = parts;. Start with common patterns: if (cronExpression.startsWith('*/')) { const interval = parts[0].split('/')[1]; return `Every ${interval} minutes`; }. Handle more patterns in the following micro-tasks.

microTasks.change: Add daily schedule pattern to cronToHuman

'PM' : 'AM'; const displayHour = h > 12 ? h - 12 : h === 0 ? 12 : h; return `Daily at ${displayHour}${m} ${period}`; }. This handles expressions like '0 7 * * *' → 'Daily at 7:00 AM' and '0 21 * * *' → 'Daily at 9:00 PM'.

'PM' : 'AM'; const displayHour = h > 12 ? h - 12 : h === 0 ? 12 : h; return `Weekly on ${dayName} at ${displayHour}:${minute.padStart(2, '0')} ${period}`; }. This handles '0 10 * * 0' → 'Weekly on Sunday at 10:00 AM'.

Fix System Monitoring page with all real data.

Project: /root/genplatform. Site: https://app.gen3.ai.

Task 1: System Health bar at top:
- Services: fetch /api/bridge/status → count running vs total
- Resources: fetch /api/bridge/metrics → show healthy if CPU<80 and RAM<80 and Disk<90
- Network: always "Connected" (the server is online if you can see this page)

Task 2: Resources tab — show ALL real metrics from /api/bridge/metrics:
- CPU Usage: percentage bar + number (real from server)
- Memory Usage: used/total in GB + percentage bar
- Disk Usage: used/total in GB + percentage bar
- Swap: used/total (if available from metrics)
- Load Average: 1min, 5min, 15min (if available)
- Uptime: real system uptime

Task 3: Services tab — show real services:
- Fetch /api/bridge/status → services array
- Each service: name, status (running/stopped), uptime, port
- Must show: OpenClaw Gateway, Bridge API, Next.js (genplatform-app)
- If service down: red indicator. If up: green.

Task 4: Logs tab — show real parsed logs:
- Fetch /api/bridge/logs → lines array
- Each log: timestamp, level (info/warn/error), message
- Color code: info=gray, warn=amber, error=red
- Auto-scroll to bottom
- "Refresh" button to reload

Task 5: Auto-refresh toggle (already exists at top right). Make sure it works:
- When ON: poll every 5 seconds for new metrics
- When OFF: stop polling
- The interval selector (5000ms dropdown) should change poll rate

After ALL: npm run build && pm2 restart genplatform-app && git add -A && git commit -m "Monitoring: all real metrics, services, logs" && git push

🔗 Preview: https://app.gen3.ai/dashboard/monitoring
Do all 5 tasks NOW.
